title: UI UX Designer
description: A UI UX Designer in India spends most of their time creating user-friendly and visually appealing digital products, such as websites and mobile applications. They conduct user research, create wireframes and prototypes, and test their designs with users to ensure a seamless user experience. They also work closely with cross-functional teams, including developers and product managers, to bring their designs to life.
top_private_colleges: Srishti Institute of Art, Design and Technology

career_progression.year_0_2: Starts as a junior designer, working on small projects and assisting senior designers. Develops basic design skills and learns to work with design tools.
career_progression.year_3_5: Takes on more responsibility, working on larger projects and leading smaller teams. Develops expertise in specific design areas, such as UI or UX.
career_progression.year_6_10: Becomes a senior designer, leading large projects and mentoring junior designers. Develops leadership skills and expertise in design management.
career_progression.entrepreneurship_exit: Can start a design studio or a product design company, offering services to clients.
career_progression.global_pathway: Can work with international companies or start-ups, or move to countries like the US, UK, or Australia for better opportunities.

india_social_context.first_year_reality: Starting salary is around ₹4-6 lakhs per annum, and the first year can be challenging as the designer needs to learn the ropes and adapt to the work environment.
common_misconceptions: Designing is just about being creative
Designers don't need to learn coding
